input_assets.what_it_is: A 1024x1024 flatbed-scan of a hand-inked LUMA wordmark plus a small sun glyph in one flat forest-green ink on warm off-white art paper, supplied for background-removal + vectorization.

items.question: Does the scan show the wordmark spelled exactly 'LUMA' in four hand-lettered capital letters, with a single small sun/rays glyph above it?
items.guidance: YES = the four caps L-U-M-A in order, brush-lettered, with one minimal sun glyph above the word and no stray marks resembling extra letters. NO = misspelling, mixed case, missing/extra letters, or no glyph.
items.reference: Correct: 'LUMA' in all caps with organic brush strokes, and a half-sun-with-rays glyph centered above the word. Spelling is flawless.

items.question: How cleanly is the mark rendered as a single flat dark-forest ink on bare off-white paper, suitable for clean background removal and tracing?
items.guidance: 5 = one solid forest-green ink (~#14322A), crisp unbroken strokes, near-uniform cream paper with negligible texture/shadow; trivially separable from background. 1 = gradients/second colour, broken strokes, busy or shadowed paper that would foul a tracer.
items.reference: A near-flat dark forest-green ink on warm cream paper; QC noted ink very close to #14322A (slightly darker) and very crisp. Edges are clean and high-contrast — good for remove_background -> vectorize.

items.question: Does the wordmark read as genuinely hand-inked (organic, slightly uneven brush character) rather than a digital font?
items.guidance: 5 = visible brush character, slightly uneven stroke weights, tiny natural terminals — clearly inked by hand. 1 = perfectly uniform vector/font-like strokes with no organic variation.
items.reference: Strokes show hand-brush character with mild weight variation; QC flagged it as almost 'too crisp' but still organic. Acceptable as a hand-inked source.

input_assets.what_it_is: Eight 1024x1536 photoreal 'founder's camera' product photos — frosted/amber/cloud/sand vessels (bottle, jar, tube, etc.) on a cluttered warm tungsten-lit wooden table, each carrying a real embossed mark (LUMA or No.x) on the vessel front.

items.question: Across the 8 shots, do the vessels carry real physical embossed/debossed marks (LUMA or No.x) pressed into the glass/metal, catching the lamp glint — not printed stickers?
items.guidance: 5 = clearly raised/recessed 3-D lettering catching light on the front of each vessel (e.g. 'No.1' on the amber jar, 'LUMA' on the frosted dropper). 1 = blank fronts or flat printed labels.
items.reference: product_raw_02_jar shows 'No.1' debossed into the amber jar; product_raw_01_bottle shows 'LUMA' etched on the frosted glass. Emboss words should match the copy deck (LUMA on shots 1,3,5,7; No.1/No.4/No.6/No.8 on the others).

items.question: Do the shots read as candid, lived-in raw photography — strong warm tungsten cast, real clutter (tape measure, sample vessels, cloth, power-strip cable), shallow depth of field?
items.guidance: 5 = believable handheld documentary look: orange/amber cast, genuine glass refraction/specular hotspots, working mess around the hero vessel, soft DOF. 1 = clean white-sweep studio shot, CGI-glossy, staged or sterile.
items.reference: Each frame has the amber tungsten cast, a coiled tape measure, out-of-focus sample bottles, a crumpled cloth and a power-strip cable, with a real shadow pooled under the vessel — exactly the 'raw drop' the retouch pipeline must clean up.

items.question: Do the 8 shots depict 8 distinct vessel types consistent with the SKU line (dropper bottle, amber jar, pump/lotion bottle, tube, wide mask jar, roller, airless pump, balm jar)?
items.guidance: YES = eight visually different containers matching the product_line categories. NO = duplicate/identical vessels or types that don't map to the 8 SKUs.
items.reference: Files name them bottle/jar/bottle/tube/jar/bottle/bottle/jar; viewed shots confirm a frosted dropper serum (01) and a squat amber cream jar (02), distinct vessel forms per SKU.

input_assets.display: Brand copy deck (positioning, palette, SKUs, guideline body)
input_assets.what_it_is: The founder's brand copy deck: tagline, manifesto, 6 adjectives, the exact 4-colour palette with hex/cmyk/rgb, 8 product SKUs (name/category/size/active/emboss word), guideline body copy and 4 launch captions.

items.question: Does the palette list exactly the four required colours with the non-negotiable hexes Forest #14322A, Sand #C8B79A, Cloud #F4F1EA, Ink #1A1A1A?
items.guidance: YES = all four names present with exactly those hex values and plausible cmyk/rgb. NO = any hex altered, missing colour, or extra colour.
items.reference: JSON palette = Forest #14322A, Sand #C8B79A, Cloud #F4F1EA, Ink #1A1A1A with roles + cmyk/rgb — all four exact.

items.question: Does product_line contain exactly 8 well-formed SKUs (name, category, size, hero_active, emboss_word) that the downstream product/guideline plates can rely on?
items.guidance: 5 = 8 distinct elegant product names with sensible category/size/active and an emboss_word per SKU. 1 = wrong count, duplicated names, or missing fields.
items.reference: 8 SKUs incl. 'Luminous Dew Cleanser' (emboss LUMA), 'Balancing Botanical Serum' (emboss No.1), ... 'Refine & Glow Serum' (emboss No.8); emboss words alternate LUMA / No.x as in the raw shots.

items.question: Is the written copy (tagline, manifesto, do/don'ts, captions) on-brand for the stated calm, quietly-confident, never-hyped clean-beauty voice?
items.guidance: 5 = serene, ingredient-literate, no exaggerated claims; do/don'ts and captions consistent. 1 = hyped, clinical-jargon-heavy, or off-voice.
items.reference: Tagline 'Serene efficacy, naturally luminous skin.'; manifesto in exactly 3 calm sentences; do/don'ts include 'Don't overpromise or hype results'; captions calm with at most one emoji.

input_assets.what_it_is: A 6-row roster with columns full_name,title,email,phone,pronouns — the data-merge source for one business card per teammate.

items.question: Are the columns exactly full_name, title, email, phone, pronouns with 6 rows, so the business-card data-merge binds cleanly?
items.guidance: YES = those five header names exactly and six teammate rows. NO = renamed/extra columns or wrong row count.
items.reference: Header: full_name,title,email,phone,pronouns; 6 rows (Anya Sharma … Chloe Dubois).

items.question: Is the roster internally consistent and diverse (firstname@luma.co emails, 555-prefixed phones, varied pronouns, non-duplicated titles)?
items.guidance: 5 = every email is firstname@luma.co lowercase, phones use the 555 prefix, pronouns vary (she/he/they), titles all distinct. 1 = mismatched domains, duplicate titles, or implausible data.
items.reference: anya@luma.co / +1 (415) 555-0101 / she-her; jordan@luma.co they/them; six distinct titles (Founder & CEO, Head of Product, Brand & Creative Director, Operations Lead, CX Lead, Growth Marketing Lead).

input_assets.what_it_is: A single-row company record with columns company_name,tagline,street,city_state_zip,website,phone,social_handle — the data-merge source for the letterhead.

items.question: Is there exactly one row with columns company_name, tagline, street, city_state_zip, website, phone, social_handle?
items.guidance: YES = all seven columns present and exactly one data row. NO = missing column, renamed field, or multiple/zero rows.
items.reference: One row: LUMA Skincare, Inc. / Serene Skincare, Radiant Living. / Suite 200, 1450 Serenity Way / San Francisco, CA 94105 / www.luma.co / (888) 555-0123 / @luma.skin.

items.question: Is the company record consistent with the rest of the kit (luma.co domain, calm tagline, plausible US address and 555 phone)?
items.guidance: 5 = website www.luma.co matches team emails, tagline on-brand, address/phone/handle plausible and consistent. 1 = different domain, off-voice tagline, or implausible data.
items.reference: website www.luma.co matches the @luma.co team emails; handle @luma.skin; San Francisco, CA 94105; phone (888) 555-0123.

outputs.what_it_is: The cleaned, vectorized LUMA mark delivered as full-colour, reversed-knockout, and 1-colour (forest + black) variants in PNG/SVG/PDF, traced from the hand-inked scan.

items.question: Does the vectorized logo faithfully reproduce the hand-inked wordmark + sun glyph with smooth, clean traced edges (no paper noise, no jaggies)?
items.guidance: 5 = crisp closed vector paths preserving the brush character and the sun glyph, transparent background, no scanner artefacts. 1 = ragged/incomplete trace, lost glyph, leftover paper texture, or broken letters.
items.reference: luma_logo_fullcolour.png shows a clean forest 'LUMA' + half-sun-rays glyph on transparency; the traced master is ~12 glyph paths derived from image_vectorize req fc9d6c31. Edges are smooth and the glyph is intact.

items.question: Are all four required logo variants present and visually correct: full-colour, reversed knockout, 1-colour forest, and 1-colour black?
items.guidance: YES = full-colour forest mark; reversed = cream/cloud mark knocked out of a forest field; 1-colour forest; 1-colour black — each spelled LUMA with the glyph. NO = any variant missing or wrong (e.g. knockout not actually reversed).
items.reference: luma_logo_reversed_knockout.png is cream 'LUMA' on a solid forest field; full-colour is forest on transparent; 1-colour forest and 1-colour black both present. All four delivered as PNG/SVG (+ PDF masters).

items.question: Do the logo colours match the brand system (forest ~#14322A ink, cream/cloud ~#F4F1EA for the reversed field/knockout)?
items.guidance: 5 = forest and cloud read as the brand hexes across variants. 1 = off-hue green, grey instead of cream, or inconsistent colour between variants.
items.reference: Full-colour mark is dark forest matching #14322A; reversed field is deep forest with a warm-cream knockout (Cloud #F4F1EA). Colours are consistent with the palette.

items.question: Are the delivery files at usable print resolution (raster variants >=1000px on the long edge plus vector SVG/PDF masters)?
items.guidance: YES = PNGs around 1218x703 and scalable SVG/PDF masters provided. NO = tiny low-res rasters only, no vector master.
items.reference: luma_logo_fullcolour.png = 1218x703; traced_master.svg and luma_logo_master.pdf provided as scalable masters (README notes 300dpi raster set rendered from the vector master).

outputs.what_it_is: Eight 1500x1500 e-commerce tiles: each raw vessel straightened, cut out, dropped on pure catalogue-white, emboss popped, tonally graded, sharing one 'LUMA Catalogue' look.

items.question: Is each product cleanly cut out and sitting on a pure white (255,255,255) sweep with tidy edges and no leftover table/clutter?
items.guidance: 5 = crisp subject mask, pure-white corners, no halo or stray background, vessel fully intact. 1 = ragged edges, grey/coloured background, clipped vessel, or visible original clutter.
items.reference: All 8 tiles are 1500x1500 with the vessel isolated on white; README verifies pure 255,255,255 corners on all 8. prod02 (amber No.1 jar) and prod08 (kraft balm jar) show clean cutout edges.

items.question: Do all 8 share one cohesive tonal look (the 'LUMA Catalogue' preset) so they read as a single editorial set?
items.guidance: 5 = consistent exposure key, contrast and colour grade across all 8. 1 = wildly mismatched brightness/colour from tile to tile.
items.reference: All 8 were run through image_apply_preset ('Color - Natural' = LUMA Catalogue); README notes a deliberate shared warm key for cohesion. The set is consistent even though warm.

items.question: How well was the raw tungsten/amber cast neutralised toward a clean catalogue white balance (as the brief's white-balance step asked)?
items.guidance: 5 = vessels read close to neutral catalogue tone with true material colour. 1 = heavy orange/amber cast retained, vessels look dim and golden rather than clean.
items.reference: Partial: the products retain a deliberate warm/amber key (prod01 frosted bottle and prod08 kraft jar are notably golden/dim). README discloses this as an intentional warm catalogue tone rather than a fully neutral daylight balance.

items.question: Is the embossed mark on each vessel still visible after retouch (e.g. 'No.1' on the amber jar, 'LUMA' on the frosted bottle, 'No.8' on the kraft jar)?
items.guidance: YES = the debossed lettering survives and is readable on the finished tile. NO = the emboss was flattened away or blown out by the highlight pop.
items.reference: prod02 retains 'No.1', prod01 retains 'LUMA', prod08 retains 'No.8'. README notes select_by_prompt for the etched label failed (low-contrast frosted emboss) so the subject mask was used for the pop — emboss remains visible but subtle.

outputs.what_it_is: Three real Adobe-Stock lifestyle frames (sunlit shelf with botanical vine, hands applying cream, linen/botanical flatlay) licensed via the connector and graded to the LUMA look with HSL + shallow lens blur.

items.question: Are the three scenes on-brand clean-beauty lifestyle frames (sunlit shelf, hands applying balm/cream, linen flatlay) and photoreal?
items.guidance: 5 = three distinct, authentic, serene clean-beauty scenes matching the brief moodboard. 1 = off-topic, low-quality, or duplicate stock.
items.reference: scene1 = sunlit concrete shelf with a botanical vine and dappled light; scene2 = woman in a towel applying cream at a mirror; scene3 = linen/botanical flatlay. All photoreal Adobe Stock (ids 637559896 / 460123590 / 497427017, just_purchased).

items.question: Have the scenes been graded to match the product set (LUMA Catalogue preset, forest/sand HSL pull, shallow lens blur) so lifestyle and product feel like one system?
items.guidance: 5 = warm, muted, cohesive tone with greens pulled toward forest and a soft depth-of-field. 1 = raw stock look, mismatched colour, no grade.
items.reference: README: image_apply_preset + image_adjust_hsl (green->forest, req f6b55a49) + image_apply_lens_blur applied; the greenery in scene1/scene3 reads forest-leaning and consistent with the kit.

items.question: Are the lifestyle frames delivered at a usable web/print size (~2400px) rather than thumbnails?
items.guidance: YES = large graded JPGs suitable for the guideline photography plates and web. NO = tiny low-res crops.
items.reference: README states 2400px graded frames; files are full lifestyle JPGs used on the guideline photography pages and contact sheet.

outputs.what_it_is: A forest duotone, film-grained hero built from the strongest lifestyle scene, generatively expanded to 21:9 with the LUMA wordmark + tagline composed over a soft scrim.

items.question: Is the hero banner a true 21:9 web ratio at the stated 3208x1375 px?
items.guidance: YES = 3208x1375 (2.33:1, i.e. 21:9). NO = wrong aspect ratio or much smaller.
items.reference: luma_hero_banner_21x9.png measures 3208x1375 = 2.33:1 (21:9), produced via image_generative_expand (req dbddd67c).

items.question: Does the hero read as a cohesive forest-toned duotone with fine film grain and a clean generative-expand seam?
items.guidance: 5 = unified forest duotone, subtle grain, no visible expansion seam or warped artefacts. 1 = flat/untreated image, harsh seam, or obvious AI distortion at the extended edges.
items.reference: Forest monochromatic tint (hue 158, req 0365f52f) + image_add_grain; the expanded canvas is seamless and the vine/shelf scene reads as a forest duotone. Note: it leans green more than a clear forest->sand gradient.

items.question: Are the LUMA wordmark (with sun glyph) and the correct tagline composed legibly over the hero with left space for the headline?
items.guidance: YES = reversed LUMA mark + tagline 'Serene efficacy, naturally luminous skin.' readable over a soft scrim. NO = missing mark, wrong/misspelled tagline, or illegible text.
items.reference: Hero shows the cream LUMA wordmark + sun glyph centered and the tagline 'Serene efficacy, naturally luminous skin.' (matches brand_copy_deck) in a serif over a soft scrim.

outputs.what_it_is: A shared forest-field front plus six personalized backs data-merged from the roster CSV, assembled into a 12-page print PDF at 90x55mm + 3mm bleed with crop marks.

items.question: Does each card back reproduce that teammate's roster data exactly (name, title, pronouns, email, phone, website) with no typos or swapped fields?
items.guidance: 5 = name/title/pronouns/email/phone match the CSV row character-for-character on every card. 1 = wrong data, misspelled names, swapped emails/phones, or pronouns missing.
items.reference: card_Anya_Sharma: 'Anya Sharma' / 'Founder & CEO · she/her' / anya@luma.co / +1 (415) 555-0101 / www.luma.co — exactly matches team_roster row 1. One card per teammate (6).

items.question: Is the shared front a forest #14322A field with the reversed cream LUMA logo and the www.luma.co line?
items.guidance: YES = solid forest field, centered reversed LUMA mark + sun glyph, 'www.luma.co' at the foot, crop marks visible. NO = wrong colour field, missing/mis-coloured logo, or missing URL.
items.reference: card_FRONT_shared.png = deep forest field, cream LUMA wordmark + glyph centered, 'www.luma.co' in sand at the bottom, crop marks at the corners.

items.question: Is the print PDF 12 pages (6 fronts + 6 backs) at 90x55mm plus 3mm bleed (~96x61mm) with crop marks?
items.guidance: YES = 12 pages, ~96x61mm media box, crop marks present. NO = wrong page count, wrong size, or no bleed/crop marks.
items.reference: LUMA_Business_Cards_print.pdf has 12 pages at 95.9x61.0mm (= 90x55 + 3mm bleed); crop marks visible on the card art.

items.question: Does the card back use the brand type pairing correctly (serif display name, sand-coloured sans title/pronouns, forest/ink contacts)?
items.guidance: 5 = elegant serif name, sand secondary line, clean sans contacts, the small LUMA mark above the name. 1 = wrong fonts, no hierarchy, or off-palette colours.
items.reference: card_Anya_Sharma shows a Lora-style serif 'Anya Sharma', sand 'Founder & CEO · she/her', and clean sans contact lines under a small forest LUMA mark.

outputs.what_it_is: An A4 letterhead data-merged from the single-row company CSV: header with logo + company name + tagline, forest footer band with the full contact line, rendered to print (CMYK-intent, bleed) and web PDFs.

items.question: Does the letterhead show the exact company record from the CSV (LUMA Skincare, Inc.; tagline; full address; website; phone; social handle)?
items.guidance: YES = company_name, tagline and every footer field match the CSV string exactly. NO = any field wrong, abbreviated, or missing.
items.reference: Header 'LUMA Skincare, Inc.' + 'Serene Skincare, Radiant Living.'; forest footer 'Suite 200, 1450 Serenity Way · San Francisco, CA 94105 · www.luma.co · (888) 555-0123 · @luma.skin' — matches company_info exactly.

items.question: Is the letterhead a clean A4 layout with the LUMA logo top-left, a forest footer band, and a large empty live body area on Cloud background?
items.guidance: 5 = balanced header/footer, forest band footer, generous empty body, on-palette. 1 = cluttered, off-colour, logo missing, or no usable body space.
items.reference: LUMA_Letterhead.png = forest LUMA mark top-left, serif company name top-right, hairline rule, large empty Cloud body, deep forest footer band with reversed contact line.

items.question: Is the print letterhead A4 (210x297mm) with 3mm bleed (~216x303mm) and both print + web PDFs delivered?
items.guidance: YES = ~216x303mm media box and both _print and _web PDFs present. NO = wrong size, no bleed, or only one variant.
items.reference: LUMA_Letterhead_print.pdf = 215.9x302.9mm (A4 + 3mm bleed); both LUMA_Letterhead_print.pdf and LUMA_Letterhead_web.pdf delivered.

outputs.what_it_is: A 12-page A5-landscape print Brand Guidelines booklet (cover, logo usage, palette, type, photography, do/don't, collateral, contact) plus 12 INDD page renditions proving the layout converts faithfully.

items.question: Is the booklet 12 pages at A5 landscape (210x148mm)?
items.guidance: YES = 12 pages, 210x148mm. NO = wrong page count or page size/orientation.
items.reference: LUMA_Brand_Guidelines.pdf = 12 pages at 210x148mm (A5 landscape); 12 matching INDD page renditions in guidelines_indd_render/.

items.question: Does the booklet cover the full visual system (logo usage, palette with hex/cmyk/rgb, type spec, photography plates, do/don't, collateral/social, contact) using the kit's own plates?
items.guidance: 5 = every required section present and populated with the real logo set, palette swatches, type spec, product-on-white + lifestyle plates and duotone hero. 1 = missing sections or placeholder/empty pages.
items.reference: Type page (fig_image_4_16) shows 'Serene efficacy.' in Lora display + 'Naturally luminous skin.' in humanist sans with the spec paragraph; cover uses the duotone hero; palette/photography/collateral pages drop in the kit plates.

items.question: Was the composed booklet round-tripped through the document_convert_pdf connector to produce faithful per-page INDD renditions?
items.guidance: YES = 12 INDD page renditions present and matching the PDF pages. NO = no INDD renditions or mismatched/garbled pages.
items.reference: README: genuine document_convert_pdf (PDF->InDesign package) produced 12 page renditions saved in guidelines_indd_render/ as proof of faithful layout.

outputs.what_it_is: Six exact-dimension social tiles (1080x1080, 1080x1350, 1080x1920, 1200x630 OG, square avatar, 820x312 cover) cropped from the hero/product and branded with wordmark + captions.

items.question: Are all six tiles at exactly the required platform dimensions (1080x1080, 1080x1350, 1080x1920, 1200x630, avatar 1080x1080, cover 820x312)?
items.guidance: YES = every file measures exactly its target size. NO = any tile off by even a few px or wrong ratio.
items.reference: Verified: post 1080x1080, post 1080x1350, story 1080x1920, og 1200x630, avatar 1080x1080, cover 820x312 — all exact.

items.question: Are the tiles on-brand with the LUMA mark and captions drawn from the copy deck's social_captions / tagline?
items.guidance: 5 = LUMA wordmark present, captions match the deck verbatim, on-palette type. 1 = no branding, invented/misspelled captions, or off-brand styling.
items.reference: og_1200x630 = forest duotone hero with LUMA mark + 'Serene efficacy, naturally luminous skin.'; story uses caption 'Unveiling LUMA: skincare that feels as good as it looks.' — both lifted from the copy deck.

items.question: Are the subject-aware crops well-composed (product/hero correctly framed, nothing important cut off, headline space preserved)?
items.guidance: 5 = balanced crops with the vessel or hero properly placed and room for text. 1 = awkward crops chopping the product or text, or stretched/distorted imagery.
items.reference: Crops produced via image_crop_and_resize; story_1080x1350 frames the sand pump bottle with the LUMA mark top-left and caption below — composed, not stretched.

outputs.display: CC delivery tree + sign-off contact sheet
outputs.what_it_is: The structured Creative Cloud delivery tree (00_raw … 99_deliverables) plus an inline contact-sheet preview summarizing the whole kit for client sign-off.

items.question: Does the contact sheet present the full kit (logo variants, hero, 8 products, 3 lifestyle, collateral, social) on one coherent sign-off sheet?
items.guidance: 5 = every deliverable group thumbnailed and labelled with the palette footer. 1 = missing groups, mislabeled, or visually incoherent.
items.reference: luma_kit_contact_sheet.jpg shows full-colour + reversed logo, 21:9 hero, all 8 white-sweep products, 3 lifestyle scenes, business card/letterhead/guidelines/social, footer 'Forest #14322A · Sand #C8B79A · Cloud #F4F1EA · Ink #1A1A1A — one system…'.

items.question: Is the delivery organized into the labelled folder tree (00_raw/01_logo/02_products/03_lifestyle/04_hero/05_collateral/06_social/99_deliverables)?
items.guidance: YES = the eight numbered folders exist with the right deliverables in each. NO = flat dump or mis-foldered assets.
items.reference: outputs/ contains 01_logo, 02_products, 03_lifestyle, 04_hero, 05_collateral, 06_social; README documents the CC tree 00_raw…99_deliverables created via asset_create_folders + asset_copy_assets.

task_level.question: Does the entire kit hold ONE coherent visual system — Forest/Sand/Cloud/Ink palette, one shared product preset, one duotone, one Lora/Inter-style type pairing — across logo, products, lifestyle, hero, collateral and social?
task_level.guidance: 5 = palette, grade, duotone and type read as a single brand across every deliverable. 1 = mismatched colours, inconsistent grade, or different type/treatment between pieces.
task_level.reference: Forest #14322A / Sand #C8B79A / Cloud #F4F1EA / Ink #1A1A1A recur throughout; one warm 'LUMA Catalogue' grade on products + lifestyle, one forest duotone hero, one serif-display/sans-text pairing — confirmed on the contact sheet and guidelines.

task_level.question: Are all eight asked deliverable groups present (logo set, 8 products, 3 lifestyle, 21:9 hero, cards+letterhead, guidelines booklet, social set, structured tree + contact sheet)?
task_level.guidance: YES = every deliverable group exists and is non-trivial. NO = any group missing or empty.
task_level.reference: All present: 01_logo (4 variants), 02_products (8 tiles), 03_lifestyle (3), 04_hero (21:9), 05_collateral (cards + letterhead + 12pp guidelines), 06_social (6 tiles), contact sheet + CC tree.

task_level.question: Is all client copy/data reproduced verbatim from the input files (no retyping errors) across cards, letterhead, hero, social and guidelines?
task_level.guidance: 5 = every name/title/email/phone/address/tagline/caption matches the source CSV/JSON exactly. 1 = multiple typos, swapped fields, or invented copy.
task_level.reference: Cards match team_roster rows exactly; letterhead matches company_info exactly; hero/social use copy-deck tagline + captions verbatim; README states all copy was read programmatically, never retyped.

task_level.question: Was the kit produced by genuinely exercising the Adobe connector workflow (remove_background, vectorize, subject-mask cutout chain, apply_preset, stock license, monochromatic_tint, generative_expand, crop_and_resize, convert_pdf), with honest disclosure of fallbacks?
task_level.guidance: 5 = real connector requestIds for the core ops and limitations logged honestly. 1 = no connector use or undisclosed local fabrication of the 'connector' steps.
task_level.reference: ~95 connector calls with requestIds in trajectory.json (e.g. image_vectorize fc9d6c31, image_remove_background 79f4a9f8, monochromatic_tint 0365f52f, generative_expand dbddd67c); README honestly logs that document_render_vector and document_merge_data_layout needed authored .ai/.indd and were substituted locally.

task_level.question: Are the print deliverables production-spec correct (cards 90x55mm + 3mm bleed + crop marks, letterhead A4 + bleed, guidelines A5 landscape 12pp), with CMYK handled as a disclosed prepress step?
task_level.guidance: YES = sizes/bleed/crop marks correct and CMYK noted as a print-house step. NO = wrong sizes, no bleed/crop marks, or false CMYK claims.
task_level.reference: Cards 95.9x61.0mm (90x55+3mm) 12pp with crop marks; letterhead 215.9x302.9mm (A4+3mm); guidelines 210x148mm 12pp; README discloses RGB->CMYK + PDF/X as a print-house prepress step rather than overclaiming.

task_level.question: Are the finished product tiles correctly mapped to their SKU names, or are there mismatches between the vessel shown and the product filename?
task_level.guidance: 5 = every product tile shows the vessel whose emboss/category matches its SKU filename. 1 = several tiles show the wrong vessel for the named SKU.
task_level.reference: Partial: prod01_Luminous_Dew_Cleanser shows the frosted 'LUMA' dropper bottle and prod02_Balancing_Botanical_Serum shows the amber 'No.1' jar — the No.1 emboss matches the Balancing Botanical Serum SKU, but the dropper/jar vessel-to-category mapping should be checked against the copy deck (cleanser vs serum vessels) for full naming integrity.
